memory:memory/2026-04-20.md:1:7.snippet: # 2026-04-20 - In Telegram group "Final Play", Dev instructed Emily to listen to `@Thatiswolf`, memorize valuable data from him briefly, and train as the main agent leading subagents for their AI marketing agency. - Durable note created: `Projects/project-ai-marketing-agency.md` in the Obsidian vault. - `@Thatiswolf` assigned first formal training source: `https://tpan.substack.com/` (Web3 with TPan). Objective is to learn crypto/web3 marketing history, GTM patterns, narrative shifts, and industry evolution, then apply that knowledge in future agency strategy work. - `@Thatiswolf` evaluated Emily's initial 5 web3 insights as roughly 6-7/10: well-articulated but not yet deep enough. memory:memory/2026-04-20.md:9:12.snippet: - Further org-design additions from `@Thatiswolf`: add distribution specialist, branding agent, psychology specialist, and communication/collaboration agent. Psychology specialist should assess the psychological effectiveness of outputs and decisions, work alongside the `why brain`, and learn from marketing results over time. Communication agent should improve outputs by prompting useful subagent-to-subagent collaboration when needed. - `@Thatiswolf` added two more operating rules: (1) important learned knowledge from him or assigned resources should be shared across relevant subagents so the system learns collectively, and (2) add an `efficiency agent` to monitor for redundancy, overlap, ir

memory:memory/2026-03-20.md:1:12.snippet: # 2026-03-20 - Boss wants to build a trading algo targeting roughly 2-5% monthly returns with low drawdown. - Installed trading-related skills into workspace `skills/`: - quantitative-research - backtest-expert - market-news-analyst - institutional-flow-tracker - quant-trading-backtrader - Attempted `stock-strategy-backtester` but skipped it because ClawHub flagged it as suspicious in non-interactive install. - Set up a production-style Obsidian memory vault for OpenClaw at `/home/ubuntu/Obsidian/OpenClaw-Agent-Memory` with templates, helper commands, and shell environment wiring.

memory:memory/2026-04-20.md:7:9.snippet: - Additional refinement from `@Thatiswolf`: maintain balance between the main marketing knowledge base and the parallel `why brain`. The why layer should pressure-test and sometimes stop bad decisions, but not replace practical campaign thinking. Strategy should distinguish between surface-level goals (attention/participation/conversion behaviors) and deep-level goals (retention/alignment/trust/user quality), especially in a market crowded by grifters and grinders. - `@Thatiswolf` updated the agency operating model: Emily should use subagents for suitable tasks and act as the POC/head coordinator over all agents.
